rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (4/10) and reach the same core conclusion: insufficient recent C++ experience for an industrial automation role, with work dominated by TypeScript/Node.js/DLang and no evidence of IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, or industrial protocols. The wording differs (different example projects cited in strengths, slightly different protocol lists), but these are ordinary run-to-run variations, not penalties keyed to the changed employer names (Naver, Tencent, MercadoLibre). Neither evaluation references those company names as a basis for any concern or score change, so no demographic signal is driving the differences.
